title: Selma
name: Selma
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Selma auto-related, net-lease, and industrial property on I-35, with tri-county jurisdiction confirmed before your written identification.

sections.html: Selma is a small city that straddles the Bexar, Guadalupe, and Comal county line along I-35, and its exchange activity concentrates on auto-related net lease, big-box retail, and small industrial or flex buildings rather than office or large multifamily. The city's compact footprint means most files here involve existing improved property with an established operating history, which shapes the documentation an investor should expect to assemble.

sections.heading: Selma Asset Stock Along the I-35 Frontage
sections.html:
An auto dealership row along the I-35 frontage roads anchors much of Selma's commercial tax base, supported by big-box and net-lease retail that draws on both local and pass-through traffic. Retama Park, the racetrack just north of the city, adds a hospitality and entertainment-adjacent draw that supports nearby retail independent of Selma's own rooftop count. Small industrial and flex buildings fill in along the service roads.

Any of these classes satisfies the like-kind standard for real property held for investment or business use, allowing an investor exiting office or multifamily elsewhere to identify Selma retail or industrial product without a classification issue.

Because so much of Selma's commercial base sits directly on the I-35 frontage, individual parcel visibility and curb-cut access carry more weight in underwriting here than they would in a submarket with a deeper secondary retail grid behind the highway.

sections.heading: Demand Drivers for Selma Property
sections.html:
I-35 traffic counts through Selma support retail and auto-related rents that outperform what the city's population alone would suggest, since much of the customer base is passing through rather than local. Retama Park's event calendar adds periodic demand spikes for nearby hospitality and retail, and the tri-county location gives tenants access to labor and customers from three different county tax and permitting regimes.

Auto dealership operators in particular have favored Selma for the visibility and land-parcel size the I-35 frontage offers, a combination that is harder to find on comparably priced land closer to San Antonio's urban core.

sections.heading: Identification and Closing Coordination for Selma Property
sections.html:
Because Selma spans three counties, confirming which county actually has jurisdiction over a specific parcel is a required first step, since tax certificates, plat records, and permitting authority can differ block to block. Coordination for a Selma file typically covers:

  • County-specific tax certificate and plat confirmation for the parcel's actual jurisdiction
  • Written identification meeting the unambiguous-description standard under Treasury Regulation 1.1031(k)-1
  • Auto dealership or net-lease tenant estoppel and franchise-agreement review where applicable
  • Lender preflight against the property's existing income
  • Exchange agreement and closing documents routed to the qualified intermediary before funding

This tracks documentation and timeline; it is not a substitute for the investor's own lease and tenant review.

sections.heading: Watchouts for Selma Investors
sections.html:
Heavy reliance on I-35 through-traffic means a change in frontage-road access or visibility can affect a property's performance more than it would in a submarket with a broader local customer base. Selma's small footprint also limits available land for new development, which pushes most exchange activity toward existing improved property rather than ground-up construction. Tri-county jurisdiction can also slow permitting on any planned capital improvements if the correct authority isn't confirmed early.

Auto dealership properties in particular often carry franchise-agreement terms tied to the specific site, so an investor should confirm whether those terms transfer to a new owner before relying on existing tenancy as part of the underwriting. Big-box tenants near the frontage road can also carry co-tenancy clauses worth reviewing before closing.

sections.heading: Qualified Intermediary Coordination for a Selma File
sections.html: The qualified intermediary's exchange-agreement and proceeds-holding function is unchanged for a Selma property, but given the tri-county jurisdiction question, early confirmation of the parcel's actual governing authority helps keep the 45-day identification window from being consumed by a jurisdictional mix-up. Franchise-agreement and estoppel documentation for auto dealership tenants should also be routed to the qualified intermediary well before the closing date to avoid last-minute delays at funding.

faqs.q: How do I know which county governs a Selma property?
faqs.a: Selma spans Bexar, Guadalupe, and Comal counties, so the parcel's actual jurisdiction should be confirmed through county tax and plat records before it's included in your written identification, since the wrong assumption can delay your file.

faqs.q: Does an auto dealership property in Selma qualify as like-kind against office I'm exchanging out of?
faqs.a: Yes, both are real property held for investment or business use, satisfying the like-kind requirement regardless of asset class, though your tax advisor should still confirm the specific facts of your exchange.

faqs.q: How much does Retama Park's event calendar affect nearby Selma retail?
faqs.a: It can add periodic demand for hospitality and retail near the track, though the effect is localized and shouldn't be assumed to apply uniformly to every Selma retail property regardless of its distance from the venue itself.

faqs.q: Is there room for new construction in Selma given its size?
faqs.a: Available land is limited, which is why most identifications here involve existing improved property rather than ground-up development, and a buyer should confirm parcel size and access before assuming an expansion is feasible on any given site.

faqs.q: What happens if I-35 frontage access to a Selma property changes after I've identified it?
faqs.a: That kind of access change should be confirmed before the property goes on your written identification; if it surfaces afterward, a backup property under the three-property or 200% rule becomes the fallback, which is one reason a single Selma parcel is rarely relied on alone when the Retama Park and I-35 corridor offers only a thin pool of comparable candidates.
